latest.title: Further Update on Fundraise & Reorganisation
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4662W
BSF Enterprise PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
("BSF" or the "Company")
Further Update on Proposed £15m Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation
Further to the Company's announcement on 27 February 2026 the Company provides an update on the previously announced Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation. Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the same meanings as in the earlier announcement unless the context requires otherwise.
The Equity Fundraise for £15m and proposed Capital Reorganisation has been mutually terminated between the parties and each party will seek to terminate all relevant agreements that formed part of the transaction.
However the CLN of £300K will continue
on the same terms but be extend for 12 months and then repaid in cash or the CLN noteholders have a right to convert over that period at next BSF capital raise price.
Further to the above, the Company confirms that it will seek alternative fundraising options and it has entered discussions with other parties to raise funds both at Group level and subsidiary level for Lab-grown Leather Ltd and Kerato Ltd.
This process remains ongoing and the Company is seeking to close a new favourable fundraising option in the coming weeks. The Company will provide further updates as appropriate.
END

latest.title: First Tannable A4-Sized Skin Achieved
latest.content:
Today 10:47
RNS Number : 0672W
BSF Enterprise PLC
10 March 2026
10 March 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
First Tannable A4-Sized Skin Achieved
Scalable Process
Unlocked for Elemental™
; T-Rex Leather™ Showcase Confirmed for 2 April 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC (LSE:BSFA), the tissue-engineering innovator, is pleased to report a significant commercial milestone for its wholly-owned subsidiary Lab-Grown Leather Limited ("
LGL
"): the successful production of a fully tanned, scaffold-free skin reaching A4 sheet size and approaching 2mm thickness - achieved in a matter of weeks using LGL's proprietary Advanced Tissue Engineering Platform ("
ATEP™
").
Key Milestones Achieved
• A4-size tannable skin produced at industrially relevant thickness (~2mm)
• Scalable process developed over the last six months using ATEP™
• Scaffold-free architecture delivering bio-equivalent durability, breathability and patina
• Zero-waste geometry: skin cultivated to specification, eliminating traditional hide scrap rates
• T-Rex Leather™ website and social channels live; showcase event confirmed for 2 April 2026
Elemental™: A New Standard for High-Performance Luxury Materials
The Elemental™ product series represents the commercial embodiment of LGL's ATEP™. Over the past six months, the LGL team has engineered a repeatable, scalable process that takes animal cells and cultivates them into a tannable skin product at dimensions that are meaningful to luxury goods manufacturers. This first full tanning of the material - a critical processing step required before leather can be used in finished goods - confirms that Elemental™ is advancing through the technical readiness pathway towards commercial supply.
Unlike many competing bio-based or synthetic alternative materials, LGL's scaffold-free approach means the cells self-organise to produce a material that is bio-equivalent to conventional hide: retaining the structural integrity, tactile properties, and characteristic patina that luxury brands demand. The process can be tuned to produce leathers that are thinner, stronger, or with unique surface characteristics that would be impossible or unethical to source conventionally. Thus LGL is able to uniquely position ElementalTM as a high-end, low-intervention material, setting the standard for a new generation of leather and cultivating a new era for luxury.
T-Rex Leather™ - Where Technology Meets Rarity
One of the most distinctive and commercially compelling applications of LGL's platform is its ability to engineer material inspired by the cellular biology of rare and extinct species. T-Rex Leather™ leverages this capability to offer the luxury and fashion industries access to materials that are, by definition, unavailable by any other means - creating a genuine scarcity narrative that is entirely authentic and ethically sound.
This positions LGL in a unique category: not simply a "more sustainable" version of existing leather, but a genuinely new class of luxury material with intrinsic rarity, provenance, and a powerful story. The T-Rex Leather™ brand has now launched its website and social media presence, and a showcase event has been confirmed for 2 April 2026, which the Company expects to generate significant press and industry engagement.
See links:
https://www.instagram.com/t_rex_leather/
https://lab-grown-leather.com/t-rex-leather/
Elemental™ skin sample - A4 size, approaching 2mm thickness
Commercial Outlook
The achievement of a tannable skin at A4 scale and industrially relevant thickness is a key gate in LGL's commercialisation roadmap. The Company is now in a position to extend its engagement with luxury goods manufacturers, material distributors, and brand partners on the basis of a tangible, scalable and demonstrable product. LGL's IP-protected ATEP™ platform provides a defensible competitive moat across both the conventional luxury leather market and the emerging exotic/extinct species segment.
The Company will continue to update shareholders on progress, including the outcome of the 2 April 2026 T-Rex Leather™ showcase event.
END

latest.title: Update on Proposed Equity Fundraise
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6080U
BSF Enterprise PLC
27 February 2026
27 February 2026
BSF Enterprise PLC
("BSF" or the "Company")
Update on Proposed Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation
Further to the Company's announcements on 28 November 2025 and 15 December 2025 (the "
Announcements
"), the Company provides an update on the proposed Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation. As previously announced, the resolutions required to implement the Equity Fundraise and Capital Reorganisation were approved at a general meeting of the Company held on 17 December 2025. Capitalised terms used in this announcement have the same meanings as in the Announcements unless the context requires otherwise.
Equity Fundraise
The Equity Fundraise remains conditional, inter alia, on approval of a prospectus to be published by the Company within the required timeframe following the entry into the relevant financing arrangements.
Further to the above, the Company confirms that it will not meet the anticipated timetable in respect of the proposed transaction and Capital Reorganisation.
The process remains ongoing and is taking longer than initially anticipated as the Company continues to assess its options and progress the required workstreams.
The Board continues to consider the proposed transaction, seeking certainty to enable it to be completed in a timely manner.
The Company will provide further updates as appropriate.
END

company_30s.what_they_do: BSFA Ltd. designs and sells high‑end leather handbags, with a flagship product called the T‑Rex that uses a patented tannable, A4‑sized skin. The brand markets itself as a boutique, luxury accessory line.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The luxury handbag market is a multi‑billion‑pound niche where margins can exceed 60 %. BSFA’s unique material and limited‑edition approach give it a defensible positioning against larger players like Coach or Mulberry.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still building its product line and raising capital. Recent filings show a reorganisation and a new supply deal, but there is no revenue yet. The share price is hovering around 0.5 p, far below the intrinsic value implied by its high‑margin business model.

rns_narrative: BSFA is actively restructuring its capital structure while simultaneously pushing forward on product development. The March update announces a new funding round and a reorganisation plan, suggesting the company is trying to shore up cash flow.
Product milestones are front‑and‑center: a new tannable skin has been achieved and a supply agreement with SeaWith indicates a path to commercial production.
Corporate governance chatter revolves around share‑count confusion and a pending share consolidation, which could clarify the market cap once resolved.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious excitement about funding and a new product line amid lingering share‑count confusion.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the fresh funding update, the first commercial skin achievement, and the SeaWith partnership as signs the company is moving from a startup phase toward production.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the share‑count mis‑reporting and the lack of any revenue to date could mean the company is still in a high‑burn, pre‑product stage with a fragile capital base.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from neutral to slightly bullish after the funding update, but the overall tone remains uncertain because of the ongoing share‑count issue.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning the market is not yet primed for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the announced equity raise – if the company raises the targeted capital, cash flow will improve and the stock could react.
Official launch of the T‑Rex handbag – a press release or product debut could spike demand and volume.
Positive AGM results – a surprise in earnings or a clear path to profitability would validate the business model.
Volume spike on a news day – a jump to >500 k shares traded would confirm a breakout.
Finalisation of the share‑consolidation – once the share count is clarified, the market will better understand the real valuation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20 % jump in volume and a break above the 0.7 p resistance. A clear statement on funding or a product launch will be the most convincing confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery; the APEX score suggests a moderate compression, but no clear mean‑reversion has yet occurred. Confirmation will come with a catalyst that lifts sentiment.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is 0.5 p, roughly 70 % below the 0.7 p support level that marks the bottom of the current crash. If the company closes the funding round and launches the T‑Rex line, the price could rebound to 1.5 p or higher, giving a 200 % return. Historically, deep‑crash stocks that recover often deliver 3‑4× the entry price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the capital raise fails or the product launch stalls, the stock could stay at 0.3 p or lower. The company’s current lack of revenue and ongoing share‑count uncertainty add downside risk. A worst‑case scenario is a 40 % loss if the price drops to 0.3 p.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200 % (0.5 p → 1.5 p). Potential downside: 40 % (0.5 p → 0.3 p). Risk‑reward ratio roughly 5:1, which is attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at 0.55 p after a volume spike of >500 k shares. Size the position so that a 0.3 p stop would represent no more than 10 % of your portfolio. Target 1.5 p with a 2‑month horizon.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a confirmed equity raise announcement or the first commercial launch of the T‑Rex handbag. Also watch for a clear share‑consolidation confirmation. Only then consider a trade.
playbook.if_passing: If the funding round is cancelled, the share‑count issue is not resolved, or the company announces a significant loss, consider abandoning the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BSFA is a deeply discounted, high‑margin luxury handbag maker that could bounce sharply once it secures funding and launches its flagship product, offering a 5:1 risk‑reward profile for contrarian traders.
